methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $144M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Atomic Machines is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jeff Holden and Prashant Patil, with $144M in total capital raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including same-stage peers Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies $288M. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-sector, same-stage comps yields $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) could not be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $245M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $894M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression yields a conservative estimate grounded in capital deployed, while peer-median captures market sentiment toward the sector but exhibits high dispersion. Valuation resolution hinges on revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to peers, and differentiation in the crowded AI-chip segment.
